# Authority Matrix

*Auto-generated by analyze_corpus.py — do not edit manually.*



# PART 1 — AUTHORITY MATRIX TABLE

| Role / Office | Action / Function | Conditions or Limits | Source AFI | Section |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Appropriate Tier Waiver Approval Authority | Approve waiver requests submitted through chain of command | Tiered compliance items only; alternately, AFGM OPR for non-tiered items | AFGM 2026-36-2033 | Waiver guidance |
| The AF (Department of the Air Force) | Approve pre-accession requests for accommodation of religious practices | Unless accommodation adversely affects military necessity (unit readiness, individual readiness, unit cohesion, good order, discipline, health, and/or safety); IAW DAFI 52-201 | AFGM 2026-36-2033 | Religious accommodation |
| Applicant's Attending Physician + Waiver Authority | Provide written approval / concurrence for postpartum members to participate in training physical requirements | Member must be participating prior to 6 months postpartum; must sign appropriate contractual statement IAW DAFMAN 36-2032 | AFGM 2026-36-2033 | Postpartum training |
| Appropriate Tier Waiver Authority / Requestor's Commander | Approve waiver requests | Tiered items to tier waiver authority; non-tiered items to requestor's commander | AFI 36-1301 | Waiver guidance |
| Appropriate Senior Level Official within the Command | Approve position and tenure waivers for key leadership positions (other than those in para 1.4.4) | Excludes PEO, DPEO, ACAT I PM/DPM, ACAT II PM positions | AFI 36-1301 | 1.4.5 / 1.7 |
| Service Acquisition Executive (SAE) | Approve selection of PEO, DPEO, ACAT I PM, ACAT I DPM, and ACAT II PM | Specific to Acquisition and Business Systems Category I and ACAT II programs | AFI 36-1301 | 1.4.4 |
| Hiring Authority | Retain final selection approval authority for key leadership positions not identified in para 1.4.4 | All other key leadership positions covered in the AFI | AFI 36-1301 | 1.4.5 |
| Service Acquisition Executive (SAE) | Approve all position waivers for PEO, DPEO, ACAT I PM/DPM, and ACAT II PM | Specific to Acquisition and Business Systems Category I and ACAT II programs | AFI 36-1301 | 1.7 |
| Appropriate Senior Level Official within the Command | Approve position waivers for all other key leadership positions | Must provide a copy of approved waiver to SAF/AQH | AFI 36-1301 | 1.7 |
| Service Acquisition Executive (SAE) | Approve waiver to release an individual from tenure agreement before it ends | Only if warranted | AFI 36-1301 | 1.7.2 |
| Service Acquisition Executive (SAE) | Approve all tenure waivers for PEO, DPEO, ACAT I PM/DPM, and ACAT II PM | Specific to Acquisition and Business Systems Category I and ACAT II programs | AFI 36-1301 | 1.7.2.1 |
| Appropriate Tier Waiver Approval Authority / CMSAF | Approve waiver requests for enlisted leader positions | Tiered items to tier authority; non-tiered items through chain of command to CMSAF | AFI 36-2109 | Waiver guidance |
| 12 Outstanding Airmen of the Year (12 OAY) | Bring spouse, family member, or one person of their choosing | One person authorized | AFI 36-2109 | 12 OAY provisions |
| Appropriate Tier Waiver Approval Authority / Requestor's Commander | Approve waiver requests | Tiered items to tier authority; non-tiered items to requestor's commander | AFI 36-2113 | Waiver guidance |
| Approval Authority (not named explicitly—context: First Sergeant career field manager) | Approve RegAF requests to serve beyond four years in the 8F000 SDI | RegAF members only | AFI 36-2113 | 2.1.14 |
| Approval Authority (not named explicitly—context: First Sergeant career field manager) | Approve RegAF applications for members currently serving in another SDI | RegAF members only | AFI 36-2113 | 2.1.15 |
| CCM + Approval Authority + WG/CC or equivalent | Approve over-grade requests during rotation | Falls under FY manning policy in effect at time; requires recommendation by CCM | AFI 36-2113 | 6.4.7 |
| AFPC/DP2OSS | Waive codes to facilitate needs of the Air Force | At AFPC/DP2OSS discretion | AFI 36-2113 | 6.8.3 |
| Appropriate Tier Waiver Approval Authority / AFPC/DPM | Approve waiver requests | Tiered items to tier authority; non-tiered items staffed through applicable MAJCOM to AFPC/DPM for final decision | AFI 36-2122 | Waiver guidance |
| Appropriate Tier Waiver Approval Authority / Publication OPR | Approve waiver requests | Tiered items to tier authority; non-tiered items to publication OPR | AFI 36-2129 | Waiver guidance |
| Appropriate Tier Waiver Approval Authority / AFPC/DP3AM | Approve waiver requests | Tiered items to tier authority; non-tiered items routed through applicable MAJCOM to AFPC/DP3AM | AFI 36-2135 | Waiver guidance |
| AFPC/DPPSP (with concurrence of AF/A1P, AF/REP, or NGB/A1P as applicable) | Approve non-tiered waiver requests | Tiered items to tier authority; non-tiered items require AFPC/DPPSP approval with concurrence | AFI 36-2406 | Waiver guidance |
| Wing Commander or equivalent (delegable no lower than Deputy Wing Commander or equivalent) | Final approval or disapproval when initial approval authority recommends disapproval | Initial approval authority must provide justification; applies when initial authority recommends disapproval | AFI 36-2406 | 1.4.11 area |
| AFPC/DPPSP | Approval authority for RegAF members requesting consideration for non-rated periods of supervision (courses) | RegAF members only | AFI 36-2406 | 1.4.11 area |
| ARPC/DPTSE | Approval authority for ARC members requesting consideration for non-rated periods of supervision (courses) | ARC members only | AFI 36-2406 | 1.4.11 area |
| Approval Authority (for criminal conviction comment waivers) | Approve waiver of mandatory comments for specific criminal convictions | Must issue written finding that mandatory comments are not in best interests of the AF and inclusion would unduly harm the ratee; decision is final and may not be appealed | AFI 36-2406 | 1.8.1.1.1.3.1 |
| Referral Reviewer | Grant extension of time for ratee rebuttal | When ratee needs additional time (e.g., non-availability of Area Defense Counsel or rebuttal returned because exceeds 10 pages) | AFI 36-2406 | (T-1) Rebuttal |
| Ratee | Grant access to completed evaluation forms | At ratee's discretion | AFI 36-2406 | 2.9.3 |
| Rater or Higher-Level Reviewer (HLR) | Use stratification statement from someone between the rater and HLR | Only when stratification comes from an intermediary between rater and HLR | AFI 36-2406 | 3.15.6.7.2 |
| AFELM/CC on G-series orders | Sign ALQ evaluations in lieu of forced distributor | Joint agencies only; exception applies when forced distributor signs the MEL | AFI 36-2406 | 4.12.5.4 |
| AFPC MLR Office | Approve exceptions to maintain integrity in the Officer Evaluation System | To ensure fair and proper consideration for all affected officers | AFI 36-2406 | MLR provisions |
| AFPC MLR Office | Approve changes to evaluation records up to one duty day prior to central selection board | Extreme circumstances only; case-by-case basis | AFI 36-2406 | MLR provisions |
| ARPC/PB | Approve changes to evaluation records up to one duty day prior to central selection board | Extreme circumstances only; case-by-case basis (ARC members) | AFI 36-2406 | ARC provisions |
| Evaluation Reports Appeal Board (ERAB) | Modify evaluations that differ from the applicant's request | May deny requested remedy and direct alternative correction (e.g., correct feedback date instead of voiding report) | AFI 36-2406 | 10.2.4 |
| AFPC or ARPC | Make corrections to digitally signed evaluations after transmission | Only AFPC or ARPC authorized once evaluation has been transmitted | AFI 36-2406 | Minor Errors |
| Chief of Staff of the Air Force (CSAF) | Approve exceptions (to evaluation/management rules) | General authority for exceptions | AFI 36-2406 | General |
| HAF Deputy Chief of Staff, Personnel | Exercise similar exception authority as CSAF | Limited to cases involving the management levels of general officers | AFI 36-2406 | General |

# PART 2 — CONFLICTING OR AMBIGUOUS AUTHORITIES

- **Non-tiered waiver routing is inconsistent across publications.** AFGM 2026-36-2033 routes non-tiered waivers to the "AFGM OPR"; AFI 36-1301 and AFI 36-2113 route them to the "requestor's commander"; AFI 36-2109 routes them to the "CMSAF"; AFI 36-2122 routes them to "AFPC/DPM via MAJCOM"; AFI 36-2135 routes them to "AFPC/DP3AM via MAJCOM"; AFI 36-2406 routes them to "AFPC/DPPSP with concurrence of AF/A1P"; AFI 36-2129 routes them to the "publication OPR." A member or commander could reasonably be confused about which non-tiered waiver authority applies for a given situation. — See AFGM 2026-36-2033 vs AFI 36-1301 vs AFI 36-2109 vs AFI 36-2113 vs AFI 36-2122 vs AFI 36-2129 vs AFI 36-2135 vs AFI 36-2406.

- **"Appropriate senior level official within the command" is ambiguous for position/tenure waiver approval for key leadership positions.** AFI 36-1301 uses this phrase for positions not covered in para 1.4.4 but does not define the specific grade, title, or organizational level that qualifies as "senior level official." This could be interpreted as a MAJCOM/CC, NAF/CC, or Center/CC depending on context. — See AFI 36-1301, paras 1.4.5 and 1.7.

- **Dual approval chain for over-grade requests during rotation.** AFI 36-2113 (6.4.7) requires a recommendation by the CCM, approval by the "approval authority," and also approval by WG/CC or equivalent. The relationship between the "approval authority" and the "WG/CC or equivalent" is unclear—whether they are the same person, sequential approvers, or alternatives. — See AFI 36-2113, para 6.4.7.

- **Overlapping authority for key leadership selection.** AFI 36-1301 para 1.4.4 assigns selection approval for PEO/DPEO/PM/DPM to the Service Acquisition Executive, while para 1.4.5 states the "hiring authority retains final selection approval authority" for all other positions. The boundary between SAE-controlled and hiring-authority-controlled positions depends entirely on the ACAT designation, which can change over a program's lifecycle, potentially creating confusion during transitions. — See AFI 36-1301, paras 1.4.4 vs 1.4.5.

- **"Approval authority" for 8F000 SDI decisions is not explicitly named.** AFI 36-2113, paras 2.1.14 and 2.1.15 state an entity "serves as the approval authority" for RegAF requests related to the First Sergeant SDI, but the subject of the sentence (likely the career field manager or a specific office established earlier in the paragraph structure) is not included in the extracted text, making the actual role ambiguous. — See AFI 36-2113, paras 2.1.14–2.1.15.

- **CSAF vs. HAF Deputy Chief of Staff, Personnel exception authority overlap.** AFI 36-2406 states "only the CSAF may approve exceptions" but then grants similar authority to the HAF DCS/Personnel for general officer management cases. Whether the DCS/Personnel authority is a formal delegation or a parallel authority is unclear, and whether there are situations involving general officers where only the CSAF (not DCS/Personnel) can act is not specified. — See AFI 36-2406, General provisions.

- **Criminal conviction comment waiver "approval authority" is unnamed at the specific level.** AFI 36-2406 describes the conditions under which a waiver of mandatory criminal conviction comments may be approved and states the decision is final and non-appealable, but the extracted text does not identify the specific role or office level that constitutes the "approval authority" for this purpose. — See AFI 36-2406, para 1.8.1.1.1.3.1.

---

# PART 3 — AUTHORITY GAPS

- **Who approves pre-accession religious accommodation requests?** AFGM 2026-36-2033 states "the AF will approve" such requests but does not designate a specific office, role, or approval authority responsible for adjudicating individual requests. The delegated decision-maker is not identified.

- **Selection and approval authority for CMSAF position.** AFI 36-2109 states that it "establishes the selection and approval authority" for the CMSAF position, but the extracted text does not identify who specifically holds that selection/approval authority.

- **Selection and approval authority for CCM and group SEL positions.** AFI 36-2109 similarly references establishing authority for these positions but does not specify the selecting/approving official in the extracted statements.

- **Documentation and/or approval authority for variable circumstances (AFI 36-2406).** The statement "The documentation and/or approval authority required will vary depending on the nature of the circumstances" acknowledges variable authority requirements but provides no matrix or decision tree for determining which authority applies under which circumstances.

- **Authority to initiate or direct evaluation corrections for minor errors prior to digital transmission.** AFI 36-2406 addresses who may correct evaluations after transmission (AFPC/ARPC only) but the extracted text does not clearly assign authority for pre-transmission corrections.

- **Authority to approve the "appropriate contractual statement" for postpartum training participation.** AFGM 2026-36-2033 references a contractual statement IAW DAFMAN 36-2032 but does not identify who has authority to validate or approve the contractual statement itself (as distinct from the medical clearance and waiver authority concurrence).

- **Final decision authority for religious accommodation denials.** AFGM 2026-36-2033 states accommodation will be approved "unless" it adversely affects military necessity, but does not specify who makes the determination that military necessity is adversely affected or who issues the denial.
